IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Tuesday, July 14, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot with **$54.75/hr** top scale and 43 calls chasing 188 hands. Meade Electric's got 23 JW slots on the Meta Data Center near Lebanon at **$53.80/hr plus 10% over scale**, running 5-12s Mon-Fri with Saturday OT. Ermco's Google play in Mooresville is 26 bodies, 5-10s plus 8-hour Saturdays. Book depth is real but the data center push keeps it moving.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** is locked in on Port Washington's data center sprawl—three contractors (Gurtz, Cupertino, MJ Electric) are running 35-20 JW calls apiece, all paying **JW wage plus $10/hr base increase**, 50-60 hour weeks, drug test locked. Valid WI license required. Pieper Electric's got healthcare flex at 18 calls if the data center grind isn't your speed.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has **$58.50/hr** top scale but Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge Data Center is paying **$44.80/hr with $100-150/day non-taxed per diem** (100+ mile radius). 45 combined calls across two Parsons tickets, 6-10s, strong incentive structure. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base offer is tighter at 10 calls but same tier pay.

**LU-1105 Newark, OH** runs 33 calls with no posted scale but THE SUPERIOR GROUP (30 JW) on Conesville is 4-10s and 1-8, most Saturdays, **all OT paid as double**. Lighthouse Electric's New Albany play is 22 calls at **$5.75/hr over scale** but mandates 45-hour minimums.

**LU-26 Washington, DC** has 40 calls, 185 hands available. JE Richards and FIS, LLC both staffing Sterling, VA data centers at M-F 8s with planned OT; FIS is posting **$125/day per diem**. Aarow Electric's Nolan Building (Springfield) is new construction, 3/4" to 4" EMT, military background preferred.

**LU-134 Chicago, IL** stays understaffed—only 165 hands against 40 calls. Elite Electric's Evanston gig is 30 JWs, 6 days/week 12 hours/day under Foreman Ron Spata.

**The play is data center saturation:** 970 open calls nationwide, but 60% are concentrated in five metros—every major contractor's stacked there, chasing the same 3,947 hands. Scale premium and per diem are becoming table stakes, not exceptions.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Tuesday, July 14, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** is the board to watch right now. New River Electric is hammering out 47 total calls (37 JL + 10 JL) on distribution work across Columbus and Southern Ohio, running 4-10s and 6-10s with **$61.44 scale**, **$125 per diem for nights stayed**, and a $100 attendance bonus if you work every available shift. JJM Power is running tighter crews on pole work near Chillicothe with **$100/day per diem** on 4-10s. Book depth sits around 109 hands — solid velocity.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has 55 inside JW calls split between Parsons Electric (45 total) and Hunt Electric at the Polaris Forge Data Center and Grand Forks Air Base. Pay is **$44.80/hr** but the real move is the incentive stacking: **$100/day non-taxed per diem Mon-Fri, $150 Sat/Sun**, plus GPS radius pushes 100+ miles. Book sits at 93 hands — that's movement.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** shows 10 calls but tight specialization. Summit, Rosendin, Cupertino, and B&D Industries are all inside JW work ranging **$40–$55/hr**, all OT double time. You'll need an NM State JIW License (EE98J) or Texas JIW. Rosendin dangles a **$15 per diem incentive for 90% of 60hr/week**. Book depth is 114 hands.

**LU-175 Chattanooga, TN** has 33 inside JW calls, mostly Lawson Electric at the DNN4 Data Center in Dalton, GA—first shift, third shift (31.4% shift differential), and shop calls. Hire-ins are Wednesday mornings. Book shows 51 hands with quick turnover.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** features 24 inside JW calls on the Micron ID1 PLA job with VECA and Quality Electric, all **$45.33/hr** base with **$170/day per diem** (7 days if eligible) and a 4-10 schedule. St. Luke's Tower work pushes **$55.33**. Book depth 50 hands.

**The market is chasing data centers and major utility grid work**—transmission, distribution, and industrial backbone projects are driving the longest boards across the Midwest and Mountain West.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Wednesday, July 15, 2026**

**LU-140 Salt Lake City, UT** is running hot with 28 calls and ~105 hands needed — the deepest board in the dispatch. BCS Mechanical's got 34 journeyman plumber slots in West Valley, plus 10 apprentice calls, and P.C. is staffing the Delta Center. DSI's looking for fitters who can TIG tack at their Draper shop. Book depth here means you can work steady if you're qualified.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** has 21 calls tight but real work — Olson's running 4/10s on Parker Advent Hospital with 4 pipefitter slots; Kiewit's got a welder gig at Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse with **$65/day per diem** on a 5/9 nightshift (5pm–2am) and a 2" super gate test requirement. US Eng. Metalworks is hiring CS MIG and SS TIG welders nightshift. Only 38 hands needed means selectivity.

**LU-213 St. John, NB** is the Irving Oil Refinery play — Stuart Olson's pushing 15 journeyman pipefitter calls on day shift 4x10s under GPMA, with smaller short-term hits from Cam and the contractor outfit at Lorneville. Steady industrial refinery work if you're mobile to the Maritimes.

**LU-290 Portland, OR** has 22 calls but they're specialized: PermaCold's hiring 3 commercial refrigeration service techs; Apollo and Charter are running BIM detailing and tool install at Intel's Ronler Acres campus. Harder's got a PVDF/PP IR welder slot in the Milwaukie fab shop.

**LU-776 Lima, OH** is fab-heavy with 8 calls across Cenovus maintenance (2 fitters, 3 combo welders) and three fab shops. BMWC is offering **$150/day per diem if over 50 miles**, plus **$11/hr shop time, $16.50/hr OT/DT within 50 miles**, and a **5% night shift premium** — do the math on those layered scales.

Industrial refinery and fab work is consolidating hands into the Mountain West and Midwest; travel per diems are sweetening the longer gigs.
